metadata.description: Official Indian Law Enforcement Uniform Codes for Scam Detection
metadata.purpose: RAG-augmented verification to detect fake police/CBI uniforms

CBI.description: CBI officers typically wear formal business attire (suits/blazers) during investigations and interrogations. They do NOT wear khaki uniforms.
key_facts: CBI officers wear formal suits or business attire, NOT khaki uniforms
CBI does NOT conduct arrests via video calls
CBI does NOT demand money during investigations
CBI officers do not wear peaked caps during interrogations
scam_indicators: Person wearing khaki uniform claiming to be CBI
Person wearing police-style peaked cap claiming to be CBI
Video call 'interrogation' - CBI does not do this

ED.description: ED officers wear formal business attire. They are financial investigation officers, NOT uniformed police.
key_facts: ED officers wear formal business suits
ED does NOT conduct arrests via video calls
ED does NOT demand immediate money transfers
ED investigations follow proper legal procedures with written notices
scam_indicators: Person in uniform claiming to be ED

IPS.description: IPS officers wear khaki uniforms with specific badges and insignia.
key_facts: IPS badge must have Ashoka Emblem (four lions)
IPS badge must have star and 'IPS' text
Peak cap is formal with specific crest, NOT baseball cap
Lanyard color varies by rank (Navy Blue for IPS, Khaki for others)

common_fakes: State initials mixed with IPS stars

collar_tabs.description: Gorget patches - dark blue with leaf structure for high-rank IPS

scam_indicators: Baseball/sports cap instead of formal peak cap
Wrong lanyard color for claimed rank
Video call 'arrest' - police cannot arrest via video

NCB.description: NCB officers may wear formal attire or specific NCB uniforms during raids.
key_facts: NCB does NOT conduct arrests via WhatsApp/Skype
NCB follows proper legal procedures with physical summons
NCB does NOT demand money for 'bail' or 'verification'
'Parcel with drugs' is a common scam narrative, not NCB procedure
scam_indicators: Threatening immediate arrest without physical summons

state_police.description: State police wear khaki with state-specific badges and insignia.
key_facts: Badge shows state name or initials (MP, DL, MH, etc.)
Different lanyard colors by state
Cannot arrest via video call
Must issue Section 41A notice for arrests

common_fakes: Wrong state badge for claimed location

scam_indicators: Claiming to be from distant state police via video

legal_facts: Indian police CANNOT arrest anyone via video call
'Digital Arrest' is NOT a legal concept in India
No agency can demand money during investigation
All summons must be in writing (Section 41A CrPC)
Judicial proceedings do NOT happen on mobile video calls
No 'supervision account' concept exists in Indian law
visual_red_flags: Blurry or printed ID card without holograms
Person in kitchen/bedroom claiming to be at police station

critical_combinations.name: CBI_khaki_scam
critical_combinations.message: FAKE: CBI officers wear business suits, NOT khaki uniforms

critical_combinations.name: video_arrest_scam
critical_combinations.message: SCAM: No Indian agency conducts arrests via video call

critical_combinations.name: digital_arrest_keyword
critical_combinations.message: CONFIRMED SCAM: 'Digital Arrest' is illegal and does not exist

critical_combinations.name: baseball_cap_officer
critical_combinations.message: SUSPICIOUS: Real officers wear formal peak caps, not baseball caps

critical_combinations.name: money_demand_during_investigation
critical_combinations.message: SCAM: No legitimate agency demands money during investigation
